meta.title: SBTI Personality Test | Free Funny Personality Quiz — 27 Types
meta.description: MBTI is outdated. SBTI is here! Answer 30 fun questions to discover your true personality type. The Controller, The Grumbler, The Dead One... 27 hilarious types await. 100% free, no sign-up.

intro.headline: MBTI is outdated.
intro.subhead: 31 questions, 1-3 minutes. A less-respectable MBTI — sorts you into one of 27 absurd personality types you can't easily explain to your parents.

q1.text: I think I'm pretty decent and worth being loved.

q2.text: If someone sincerely told you "You're really amazing," what's your gut reaction?

q3.text: If you had to sum up "what kind of person I am" in a few sentences, could you do it clearly?

q4.text: I have something I truly aspire to deep down.

q5.text: I'm willing to invest real time and energy into things I genuinely believe in.

q6.text: Have you ever seriously done something that wasn't for money or praise, purely because you felt it was the right thing to do?
options: Maybe out of obligation or social pressure.
I'd rather people not find out I'm secretly a dark person.

q7.text: Imagine you're in a relationship. Do you think a healthy romance means both people should maintain independent identities?

q8.text: Your partner/friend says they can't make your plans today because they've "had diarrhea for 5 hours straight."
options: Nobody has diarrhea for 5 hours. They're hiding something.
Maybe they're genuinely feeling awful today.

q9.text: If you met someone you really clicked with, what would you do?
options: No matter how great they are, I wouldn't fall too deep.
Somewhere in between A and C.
I'd treasure them so much I might go full lovesick puppy.

q10.text: A little girl you've never met walks up and hands you a lollipop.
options: Omg she's so sweet! She gave ME a lollipop!
Is this some new type of scam? Better walk away.

q11.text: Do you frequently check your partner's/close friend's social media to see if they're chatting or interacting with others?

q12.text: Imagine you're in a relationship. Your partner says they want to be together 24/7 and share absolutely everything. How do you feel?
options: I'd rather keep some personal space

q13.text: Do you believe most people are fundamentally good at heart?
options: There are more evil hearts out there than hemorrhoids in the world.
Yes, I choose to believe there are more good people.

q14.text: Do you think the world is generally trustworthy, or full of hidden traps?

q15.text: Before doing something, do you make detailed plans, or do you prefer winging it?

q16.text: I make detailed plans in advance and strictly follow through on them.
options: Plans never survive contact with reality.
Sometimes I finish them, sometimes I don't.
I hate when my plans get disrupted.

q17.text: I feel like my life has a clear sense of meaning and direction.

q18.text: Would you agree that you're currently living the life you actually want?

q19.text: When doing something, is your core motivation more "I want to win / do better" or "don't mess up / don't embarrass myself"?

q20.text: Do you agree: I'm a highly driven and ambitious person.

q21.text: When it comes to making quick decisions, do you find yourself overthinking every angle in your head?

q22.text: You've been sitting on the toilet for 40 minutes and nothing's happening. What do you do next?
options: Sit for another 30 minutes. Maybe something will happen.
Slap your own butt and yell: "Come on, just go already!"
Use a suppository. Let's get this over with.

q23.text: Imagine you're a student with exams coming up, and a close friend invites you on an out-of-town trip. What do you do?
options: Might as well ask for a day off.
Exams are coming, are you kidding me?

q24.text: Are you the type who only unlocks god-tier productivity when the deadline is breathing down your neck?
options: When backed into a corner, my execution is honestly insane...
Yes, things are meant to be pushed forward proactively.

q25.text: At a party full of strangers, would you go up and start conversations?
options: I talk big online, but in person I get kinda nervous.
Meeting new people is fine, I'll chat if someone comes to me.
I'd dress up and chat enthusiastically. You never know, right? I mean, you never know!

q26.text: I'm a proactive and outgoing person in social settings.

q27.text: Your friend brings along their friend. How do you treat this "friend of a friend"?
options: I naturally keep some distance with friends-of-friends, worried it might complicate things.
Depends on the vibe. If we click, we click.
A friend's friend is basically my friend too! Time to chat it up!

q28.text: Sometimes what you say and what you actually think aren't quite the same.

q29.text: Do you act noticeably different in various social settings (work, friends, family)?

q30.text: Hosaka Shinichi, I used to think you were interesting, maybe even cool, but actually you're just a boring person. Really. Starting today, I never want to see your face again. Got it? You're fired.

drink_gate_q2.text: Do you agree with the following statement?
options: I drink occasionally, but not much.
I keep liquor in a thermos and sip it like water. Alcohol speaks to my soul.

CTRL.name: The Puppeteer
CTRL.intro: Got you wrapped around my finger, didn't I?
CTRL.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest personality type in all of China. You are the natural rebel against the universe's law of entropy! 99.99% of so-called "successful people" worldwide are just cheap knockoffs of you. The CTRL personality is a walking, talking, self-propelled task manager in human form. What ordinary people call "rules" are just factory default settings to you; what mortals call "plans" are merely idle doodles you scribble on a napkin. What does having a CTRL friend mean? It means your life's GPS system just got a serious upgrade. Because CTRL is the master of getting people in line. CTRL will hit Ctrl+S on your life one second before your train goes off the rails, then drag you back on track with a logic so airtight you can't refuse. They're the last backup drive of your chaotic existence, the only reboot button still glowing in the dying light of the universe.

ATM-er.name: The ATM
ATM-er.intro: You think I'm made of money?
ATM-er.desc: Congratulations, you've somehow tested into the world's rarest personality type. You may become the great unsolved mystery of the finance world -- yes, the ATM-er doesn't necessarily "give away money," but they're perpetually paying. Paying time, paying energy, paying patience, paying away a perfectly peaceful evening. Like a battered but indestructible ATM machine, people insert their anxiety and problems, and out comes a reassuring "Don't worry, I got this." Your life is one grand, unsung, solo act of picking up the tab. You somehow absorb an avalanche of demands with the reliability of bedrock, only sighing at the bill -- perhaps a spiritual one -- in the dead of night: Oh, this damn, homeless sense of responsibility I can't seem to shake.

Dior-s.name: The Loser
Dior-s.intro: Just wait for my glow-up arc.
Dior-s.desc: Congratulations! You're not actually a loser -- you're the long-lost spiritual heir of Diogenes, the OG cynic philosopher, because Dior-s actually stands for Diogenes' Original Realist - sage. The Dior-s personality is the ultimate middle finger to modern consumerism and hustle-culture brainwashing. They're not "unmotivated" -- they've already seen through the fact that all that "grinding" leads to is a fancier prison cell. Dior-s carries serious wisdom. While everyone else chases trends and gets slapped around by the waves of the times, Dior-s is already sunbathing in their philosophical barrel, having achieved the supreme state of barrel-human unity. Their creed isn't empty talk -- it's a set of laws verified by billions of trials of physics and biology: First, lying down is more comfortable than standing. Second, when it's mealtime, you eat.

BOSS.name: The Boss
BOSS.intro: Hand me the steering wheel. I'm driving.
BOSS.desc: BOSS is someone whose hands are permanently glued to the steering wheel. Even when the gas tank is flashing red, even when the GPS is talking nonsense, they'll say with a straight face: I'll drive. And then they actually get the car to the destination. This personality type operates on its own physics -- the Law of Eternal Upward Momentum. The BOSS personality looks at the world like a player who's 100%-completed the game watching a noob tutorial. Efficiency is their religion, order is their oxygen. They don't "naturally radiate leader energy" -- they ARE the human-shaped charisma generator. Within a five-meter radius, the air itself automatically becomes serious and productive. What they call "self-improvement" is what normal people call "self-torture." Today they master a new language, tomorrow they get a professional certification, the day after they're planning to colonize Mars. You say that's too intense; they look at you like you're a baby chick: I'm not being harsh. You're just soft.

THAN-K.name: The Gratitude Maniac
THAN-K.intro: I thank the heavens! I thank the earth!
THAN-K.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest personality type in all of China. And you should thank ME for it! Be grateful that you've been blessed with life's nourishment at this very moment! If you get stuck in traffic on the way to work? You should say: I'm grateful for this traffic jam -- it gave me more time to enjoy this lovely song and admire every anxiety-twisted face outside the window, making me cherish my inner peace even more. Yes, THAN-K has a personality as warm as jade and a heart as vast as the ocean. In their eyes, there are no truly bad people, only "friends who haven't yet been reached by the light of gratitude." Having a THAN-K friend is like having an inexhaustible positive-energy broadcast tower by your side. They could literally help you spot a Van Gogh-style Starry Night in the mold growing in the corner of your bathroom.

OH-NO.name: The Oh-No Person
OH-NO.intro: Oh no! How did I get THIS personality?!
OH-NO.desc: "Oh no!" is not a scream of fear -- it's a form of supreme intelligence. When a normal person sees a cup on the edge of a table, the Oh-No Person sees a disaster epic composed of "water stain - short circuit - fire - building evacuation - economic losses - butterfly effect - end of the world." And so, with an "Oh, no!" erupting from the depths of their soul, they move the cup to the exact center of the table at lightning speed, then place a coaster underneath for good measure. The Oh-No Person has an almost obsessive respect for boundaries: yours is yours, mine is mine. Every accident and risk has already been strangled in its cradle by their perfectly timed "Oh, no!" They are the guardian deities of order, the last batch of respectably uptight people holding it together in this chaotic world.

GOGO.name: The Go-Getter
GOGO.desc: Research has revealed that the GOGO personality's brain is fundamentally wired differently from the average human. GOGO lives in an extreme "what you see is what you get" world, with a life philosophy so brutally simple it's almost offensive: If I close my eyes, it's dark outside. If I spend all my money, I'm broke. If I'm standing on a crosswalk, I'm a pedestrian. Flawless logic. Completely irrefutable. While others are still debating "which came first, the chicken or the egg," GOGO has already turned both into a "Chicken-and-Egg Ultimate Truth Rice Bowl." They're not "solving problems" -- they're "clearing the to-do list." In their world, there are only two states: Done, and About To Be Done By Me.

SEXY.name: The Stunner
SEXY.desc: When you walk into a room, the lighting system automatically identifies you as a natural-born stunner and dims the lights to avoid wasting energy. When you smile, you become a smiling stunner, and the surrounding humidity drops noticeably because all the water vapor condenses into little hearts in people's eyes. No matter who it is, they can't help but pay an unreasonable amount of attention to your existence. Legend has it that if enough SEXY personalities gather for a party, the combined charm energy released could temporarily warp the fabric of spacetime, creating a blissful illusion of "time slowing down" for all attendees. They don't need to try hard to express themselves -- most of the time, their mere existence already reads like an extravagantly gorgeous prose poem.

LOVE-R.name: The Hopeless Romantic
LOVE-R.intro: So much love to give, reality feels a little barren.
LOVE-R.desc: The LOVE-R personality is like a rare species that somehow survived from the age of mythology. Their odds of existing are lower than the odds of you fishing the author's arm out of a toilet. You are basically the last and most gloriously out-of-place troubadour of this cold, steel-and-concrete age. Your emotional processor doesn't run on binary -- it runs on rainbows. A falling leaf, which to most people just means "autumn's here," is for LOVE-R a thirteen-act tragicomedy about reincarnation, sacrifice, and wordless love. Your inner world is like a theme park that never closes. You spend your whole life searching for that one soulmate who can read the park map and is willing to ride the carousel with you until the end of the universe.

MUM.name: The Mom
MUM.intro: Could I... maybe call you Mom...?
MUM.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest Mom personality in all of China. Yes, before chaos took form and time had no name, before the first star let out its first burp, there was already Mom. The Mom personality's core color is gentleness. They excel at reading emotions, possess superhuman empathy, and know when to stop, when to tell themselves "let it go." Mom is like a doctor, healing other people's sadness. The only catch is -- when Mom cries, the dose of medicine they give themselves is always a size smaller than what they give everyone else. MUM's gentleness toward themselves always comes at a discount.

FAKE.name: The Faker
FAKE.intro: Humanity is extinct. Only fakers remain.
FAKE.desc: SCP Foundation Emergency Report: Item Number SCP-CN-xxxx "The Shapeshifter." In social settings, the Shapeshifter is an absolute chameleon -- they switch personality masks faster than you switch keyboard languages. One second they're in ride-or-die bestie mode, the next a boss walks in and they instantly toggle to dependable-model-employee mode; even the sheen and curl of their expression subtly recalibrates. You think you've made a friend who truly gets you? Wake up. You just got lucky enough to encounter a high-performance, expertly disguised android, that's all. Late at night, the Shapeshifter peels off their masks one by one, only to discover that underneath... it's empty. Those masks were the self all along.

OJBK.name: The IDGAF
OJBK.intro: I said I don't care, and I meant it.
OJBK.desc: Let's confront the raw energy of this name: OJBK. This is no longer a personality type -- it's a governing philosophy. When mere mortals face the century's greatest dilemma of "rice or noodles for lunch," their brains burn through calories in agonized deliberation. But the OJBK personality, with the detached calm of an emperor reviewing court petitions, will casually drop two words: Either's fine. This isn't indecisiveness -- this is telling you: your mortal choices mean nothing to me, peasant. Why no arguments? Because debating the future of the universe with an amoeba is pointless. Why no strong opinions? Because an emperor doesn't care whether the dust at their feet drifts left or right.

MALO.name: The Monke
MALO.intro: Life's a dungeon, and I'm just a little monkey.
MALO.desc: Friend, you're not "young at heart" -- you straight up never evolved. Your soul is still stuck in that glorious era of swinging from branches and losing your mind over bananas. When humanity's ancestors decided to climb down from the trees, learn to walk upright, and put on suits and ties, the Monkey Brain's ancestors were sitting on the neighboring branch watching them, scratching their butt, and letting out a dismissive "tsk." They saw through it all: so-called "civilization" is just the most boring, least fun pay-to-play game ever made. Rules are meant to be bent, ceilings are meant to be hung from upside-down, conference rooms are meant for backflips. MALO is basically a wild idea that fell out of a massive plot hole and forgot to close the door behind it.

JOKE-R.name: The Clown
JOKE-R.intro: Turns out we were all clowns.
JOKE-R.desc: Please note: the JOKE-R personality is not a "person" so much as a clown wearing jokes as clothing. Peel back one layer -- it's a joke. Peel back another -- it's a bit. You keep peeling, layer after layer, until finally at the very center... it's empty. Just a faint echo whispering: Ha, didn't see that coming, did you? JOKE-R is the official Head of Vibes and sole designated DPS of every social gathering. With them around, the party never dies. Everyone's doubled over laughing, and the one laughing hardest is usually JOKE-R themselves -- using the loudest laughter to drown out the sound of their heart breaking.

WOC!.name: The WTF Person
WOC!.intro: WTF, how did I get this personality?
WOC!.desc: We've discovered a magnificent creature -- the WOC! Person. They run on two completely independent operating systems: one called the "Surface System," responsible for generating exclamations like "WTF," "no way," and "huh?!" in rapid succession; the other called the "Backend System," responsible for calmly analyzing: Yep, just as I expected. The WOC! Person only says WTF -- they never meddle in other people's business, because they know full well that explaining logic to a fool is like trying to prop up mud against a wall: it wastes your energy and you just end up with crap on your hands. So they choose to clutch their blade of grass of wisdom and offer a heartfelt "WOC!" as the highest tribute to this insane world.

THIN-K.name: The Overthinker
THIN-K.desc: Research has revealed that the THIN-K personality's brain is fundamentally wired differently from the average human. As the name suggests, your brain is perpetually in thinking mode. You're a master at interrogating information -- scrutinizing arguments, evidence, logical reasoning, potential biases, and even demanding a "three-generation ideological background check on the author." In this age of information overload, you never blindly follow. You weigh pros and cons in relationships and fiercely guard your personal space. When others see you sitting alone and assume you're spacing out? Fools. That's not spacing out -- that's your brain running a full sort, archive, and purge cycle on every piece of information received today.

SHIT.name: The Bullshitter
SHIT.intro: This world is a steaming pile.
SHIT.desc: Congratulations, the SHIT personality is the one and only known rare personality type in the universe. The word "shit" here isn't complaining -- it's a sacred ritual. SHIT's behavioral pattern is one epic, earth-shattering paradox. Mouth: This project is absolute garbage. Hands: Quietly opens Excel, starts building formulas and Gantt charts. Mouth: These coworkers are all useless. Hands: After a colleague screws up, stays up all night cleaning the mess until it's spotless. Mouth: This world is total crap, just end it already. Hands: Wakes up at 7 AM the next morning, squeezes onto a crappy subway, goes to a crappy job. Don't worry -- that's not the apocalypse alarm. That's the charge horn signaling they're about to save the world.

ZZZZ.name: The Playing-Dead Pro
ZZZZ.intro: I'm not dead, I'm just sleeping.
ZZZZ.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest playing-dead personality in all of China. You can ignore 99+ unread messages in a group chat without batting an eye, but when someone drops the nuclear option -- "@everyone 30 minutes left before deadline" -- you rise from your thousand-year slumber like an ancient mummy, slowly type out "Got it," and then in the next 29 minutes deliver a submission that is... technically passing. Yes, it's only when the Deadline -- that one supreme, highest-clearance command -- arrives that you truly awaken. Silent until the moment you roar. You've proven a universal truth to the cosmos: sometimes doing nothing means you can't do anything wrong.

POOR.name: The Broke Specialist
POOR.intro: I'm broke, but I go hard.
POOR.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into [POOR - The Broke Specialist]. This "poverty" isn't your bank balance's death sentence -- it's more like a Marie Kondo purge of desire followed by aggressive resource reallocation. While others spray their energy around like handing out QR codes on a street corner, you compress yours into a laser beam -- wherever you point it, things start smoking. POOR's world is simple: everything unimportant gets noise-cancelled, everything important gets absolutely hammered. Socializing, clout-chasing, showing off everywhere? Sorry, no bandwidth. You're not resource-poor -- you've poured every last drop into a single mine shaft. It looks like poverty from the outside, but it's actually a gold mine. Once you decide something's worth drilling into, the outside world becomes background static.

MONK.name: The Monk
MONK.desc: While others seek enlightenment about love and heartbreak at karaoke, the MONK personality chooses to contemplate the Great Way at home. MONK has already seen through the material world and has no interest in strangers disturbing their meditation or breaking their spiritual cultivation. MONK's personal space is their force field, their sacred mountain, their absolute domain -- inviolable. Trespassers will feel a suffocating pressure emanating from the depths of the soul. MONKs don't cling and don't entangle, because in their worldview, all things orbit on independent paths. Planets maintain billions of miles of distance between each other to form a harmonious universe -- why can't people do the same?

IMSB.name: The Dumbass
IMSB.intro: Wait... am I actually a dumbass?
IMSB.desc: Congratulations! You don't even fall within human classification! You've tested into the once-in-a-million-years [IMSB] personality. Inside the IMSB brain live two immortal warriors locked in eternal combat: one called "Let's GOOOO!" and the other called "I'm such an IDIOT!" When IMSB encounters someone they're attracted to, Warrior #1 says: Go! Ask for their number! Ask them to dinner! Say it loud! Then Warrior #2 chimes in: Why would they ever be into YOU? Going over there is just asking for humiliation! Final result: stares at the person's back until they disappear, then pulls out phone and googles "how to overcome social anxiety." IMSB isn't actually dumb -- it's just that your internal monologue is probably longer than the entire Marvel Cinematic Universe combined.

SOLO.name: The Orphan
SOLO.intro: I'm literally crying... why am I the orphan??
SOLO.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest [SOLO - Orphan] personality in all of China. Don't cry just yet -- a king's coronation ceremony is usually a solo affair. The Orphan's self-worth tends to run low, so they sometimes push people away preemptively. They've built a Great Wall around their soul called "Don't Touch Me." Every brick is a scar from the past. The Orphan is like a hedgehog that's hidden all its soft spots and pointed every spine at the world. Those thorns aren't an attack -- they're a thousand unspoken sentences of "don't come closer, I'm afraid you'll get hurt too" and "please, I'm begging you, don't leave."

FUCK.name: The Savage
FUCK.intro: F*CK! What kind of personality is this?
FUCK.desc: Congratulations! You don't even fall within human classification! You've tested into the once-in-a-million-years [FUCK] personality. In the city of human civilization, there has appeared an indestructible, herbicide-proof, superpowered human-shaped weed -- that is the Savage personality. Its scientific name is simply FUCK. In FUCK's worldview, social conventions are utterly meaningless, and FUCK's emotional switch is a physical toggle with exactly two positions: FUCK YEAH and FUCK OFF. FUCK doesn't just chase the rush of the moment -- they're chasing a raw, untamed life force crashing around inside them. When everyone else has been domesticated into tame house pets, FUCK is the last wolf howl echoing across the wilderness.

DEAD.name: The Dead Inside
DEAD.desc: Congratulations, you've tested into the rarest personality type in all of China. It's just that "The Dead" is a rather unfortunate name, so you can also call it: Don't Expect Any Drives. The Flatline has already seen through all that meaningless philosophical navel-gazing, which is why they seem to have "lost interest" in everything. The way they look at the world is like a top-tier gamer who's beaten every main quest, side quest, and hidden mission, deleted and restarted 999 times, and finally realized: this game was never fun to begin with. The Flatline is the ultimate sage who has transcended desire and ambition. Their very existence is the most silent yet most thorough protest against this noisy world.

IMFW.name: The Useless
IMFW.desc: Congratulations, what you've tested into isn't an ordinary personality -- you are an extremely rare species, comprising only 0.0001% of the world's population: [The Useless]. The Useless tend to have fragile self-esteem, lack a sense of security, and sometimes lack a strong sense of self, which is why they can precisely detect the strongest WiFi signal in any room -- meaning the person they trust most in their heart. Stepping into their life is like entering a premium orchid greenhouse: you need to precisely control the temperature, humidity, and administer daily "I love you" verbal photosynthesis sessions. Give them a piece of candy, and they'll return a look of pure, sparkling, unconditional trust. You're not actually useless -- you're just too unguarded, too quick to care for real.

HHHH.name: The Giggler
HHHH.desc: Congratulations! Because your brain's wiring is so wonderfully bizarre, the standard personality database has completely crashed. When the top personality match falls below 60%, the system force-assigns you this type -- [HHHH - The Giggler]. What traits does this personality have? Hahahahahahahahaha! Sorry, that IS the entire trait set. You can check the 15-dimension breakdown for a very unprofessional assessment. The author sincerely apologizes -- they didn't think of everything when designing the personalities, which is why this situation exists. Hahahahaha... laughing and laughing, I started crying. How does someone's brain work like this.

DRUNK.name: The Drunk
DRUNK.intro: Liquor burns the throat. No choice but to get wasted.
DRUNK.desc: Why do you walk like the floor is moving? Why are your emotions permanently set to turbo? Why is everything you see in double vision? Because what flows through your veins isn't blood -- it's premium baijiu! It's top-shelf liquor! It's bargain booze! It's every bottle ever made! Oh, glorious alcohol, every drop burning, every drop boiling. Have you already gotten into the habit of pouring hard liquor into a thermos and chugging it like water? What magnificent booze! It makes you the life of every dinner table and then makes you hug the toilet confessing your life sins afterward. It makes you believe you're the poet of the night, the inextinguishable flame at the center of the universe -- until 10 AM the next morning, when your head feels like a cracked walnut, food residue crusted at the corner of your mouth, your soul cowering in a corner. You finally understand: that loudmouth who was pontificating and slamming tables last night has officially become a drunkard.

S1.L: You're harder on yourself than anyone else could be. Someone compliments you and your first instinct is to check if they're being sarcastic.
S1.M: Your confidence fluctuates with the weather -- soaring when things go well, shrinking the moment they don't.
S1.H: You've got a pretty solid read on yourself. A random stranger's offhand remark isn't going to rattle you.

S2.L: Your inner channel is mostly static -- frequently buffering in the "who even am I" loop.
S2.M: Most days you can recognize yourself, though emotions sometimes hijack the account temporarily.
S2.H: You've got a clear handle on your temperament, desires, and hard limits.

S3.L: You prioritize comfort and safety -- no need to run life in sprint mode every single day.
S3.M: Part of you wants to grind, part of you wants to nap. Your inner values committee is always in session.
S3.H: You're easily propelled forward by goals, growth, or some deeper conviction that matters to you.

E1.L: Your relationship alarm system is hypersensitive -- a left-on-read can spiral into a full series finale in your head.
E1.M: Half trust, half testing -- emotionally you're constantly seesawing.
E1.H: You'd rather believe in the relationship itself. A little breeze isn't going to send you running.

E2.L: Emotionally reserved. It's not that the door is closed -- the access control is just really strict.
E2.M: You invest, but you always keep a reserve. Never going all-in at the poker table.
E2.H: Once you're in, you're ALL in. Your emotions and energy get served up in generous portions.

E3.L: You tend to cling and get clung to -- emotional warmth in relationships is your oxygen.
E3.M: You want closeness AND independence, adjusting the dial as you go.
E3.H: Personal space is sacred. Even head-over-heels in love, you keep a piece of turf that's yours alone.

A1.L: You see the world through a defensive filter -- suspect first, approach second.
A1.M: Neither naive nor full conspiracy theorist. Watching from the sidelines is your default.
A1.H: You lean toward trusting humanity and goodwill. Not rushing to declare the world guilty.

A2.L: Rules are more like guidelines -- comfort and freedom tend to rank higher.
A2.M: You follow rules when it matters and bend them when it doesn't. Flexible pragmatism.
A2.H: Strong sense of order. If there's a process, you'd rather stick to it than improvise.

A3.L: Sense of purpose running low -- a lot of things feel like you're just going through the motions.
A3.M: Sometimes you've got a goal, sometimes you want to just veg out. Life's operating system is on standby.
A3.H: You move with direction. You have a decent idea of which way you're headed.

Ac1.L: Your risk-avoidance system boots up before ambition does. "Don't crash" outranks "go fast."
Ac1.M: Sometimes you want to win, sometimes you just want to not get hassled. Mixed fuel.
Ac1.H: You're easily lit up by results, growth, and the feeling of making progress.

Ac2.L: Before deciding, you spin in circles. The committee in your head always runs overtime.
Ac2.M: You think, but not to the point of system crash. Normal-grade hesitation.
Ac2.H: You make the call fast and don't like looking back to second-guess.

Ac3.L: Your execution and deadlines have a deep emotional bond -- the later it gets, the more you awaken.
Ac3.M: You can get things done, but your output depends on the vibe. Sometimes steady, sometimes stalling.
Ac3.H: Strong drive to push things forward. Unfinished business feels like a splinter in your brain.

So1.L: Social engine takes a while to warm up. Making the first move usually requires a serious pep talk.
So1.M: If someone comes to you, great. If not, you're not forcing it. Average social elasticity.
So1.H: You're the one who breaks the ice. You don't mind sticking your head out in a crowd.

So2.L: You lean toward closeness and merging. Once you vibe with someone, they're basically inner circle.
So2.M: You want to be close but also keep a gap. Boundaries flex depending on who it is.
So2.H: Strong boundary game. When someone gets too close, your instinct is to take half a step back.

So3.L: You express directly. Whatever's in your head mostly comes out unfiltered.
So3.M: You read the room before speaking. Authenticity and diplomacy each get a slice.
So3.H: Skilled at switching modes for different contexts. Your real self gets revealed in layers.

footer.tagline: A personality test that doesn't take itself seriously.

share.tweetText: I got {code} ({name}) on the SBTI personality test! What's your type?

about.metaTitle: About SBTI — What Is the Satirical Behavior Type Indicator?
about.metaDescription: Learn about SBTI, the satirical personality test that parodies MBTI with 27 hilarious personality types across 5 behavioral dimensions.
about.whatIsSbtiP1: SBTI (Satirical Behavior Type Indicator) is a humorous personality test that lovingly parodies traditional personality assessments like MBTI. Instead of pretending to unlock the secrets of your psyche, SBTI embraces the absurdity of trying to fit complex humans into neat little boxes — and makes it entertaining.
about.whatIsSbtiP2: With 27 uniquely ridiculous personality types — from CTRL (The Puppeteer) to DEAD (The Detached) — SBTI gives you a fun, shareable label that's more honest than any serious test could ever be.
about.howStep1Desc: Quick, fun questions about your daily behaviors and preferences. No right or wrong answers.
about.howStep2Desc: Our algorithm matches your responses to one of 27 personality types based on 15 behavioral dimensions.
about.howStep3Desc: Discover your type's full profile, compare with friends, and share your results on social media.
about.dimensionsIntro: SBTI measures personality across 5 behavioral models, each with 3 sub-dimensions, totaling 15 dimensions.
about.dimSDesc: How you see yourself — self-esteem, clarity, and core values.
about.dimEDesc: How you connect emotionally — attachment, investment, and boundaries.
about.dimADesc: How you see the world — optimism, flexibility, and sense of purpose.
about.dimAcDesc: How you get things done — motivation, decision-making, and execution.
about.dimSoDesc: How you navigate relationships — initiative, boundaries, and authenticity.

faq.metaTitle: SBTI FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions About the Personality Test
faq.metaDescription: Find answers to common questions about SBTI: how the test works, what the 27 types mean, how scoring works, and more.
faq.a1: SBTI (Satirical Behavior Type Indicator) is a humorous personality test that parodies traditional assessments like MBTI. It features 27 uniquely absurd personality types that are designed to make you laugh while offering surprisingly accurate behavioral insights.
faq.q2: How many personality types are there?
faq.a2: There are 27 main personality types, plus 2 hidden special types that can be unlocked through specific answers. Each type has a unique code, name, and detailed personality description.
faq.q3: How does SBTI differ from MBTI?
faq.a3: While MBTI uses 4 binary dimensions (16 types), SBTI uses 15 dimensions across 5 behavioral models with 3 levels each (Low/Mid/High). More importantly, SBTI doesn't pretend to be scientific — it's designed to be fun, shareable, and surprisingly insightful.
faq.a4: Yes, SBTI is completely free. No sign-up required, no email collection, no premium results behind a paywall. Just take the test and get your full results instantly.
faq.q5: How long does the test take?
faq.a5: The test has 30 questions with 3 options each. Most people complete it in 3-5 minutes.
faq.a6: The 5 models are: Self-Perception (how you see yourself), Emotional Style (how you connect), Attitude & Worldview (how you see the world), Action Patterns (how you get things done), and Social Dynamics (how you navigate relationships). Each model has 3 sub-dimensions.
faq.a7: Absolutely! You can retake the test as many times as you want. Your answers are processed locally in your browser and are never stored on any server.
faq.a8: No. SBTI is a fully static website. All quiz logic runs in your browser. We don't collect, store, or transmit your answers. The only analytics we use is Google Analytics for page views.
faq.q9: What languages is SBTI available in?
faq.a9: SBTI is available in 4 languages: Chinese (中文), English, Japanese (日本語), and Korean (한국어).
faq.a10: Yes! Each personality type has its own shareable page with a unique URL. You can share your results on Twitter/X, Facebook, or copy the link to share anywhere.

typesIndex.metaTitle: All 27 SBTI Personality Types — Complete Type Directory
typesIndex.metaDescription: Browse all 27 SBTI personality types: from CTRL (The Puppeteer) to DEAD (The Detached). Find your type and explore detailed profiles.
typesIndex.pageSubtitle: 27 uniquely absurd types. Which one are you?
typesIndex.headTail: , which one is hiding in your group chat?
typesIndex.intro: 31 questions sort you into one of 27 absurd types you can't really explain to your parents. Below is the full cabinet — every drawer is a real specimen we've pulled. One of them is probably you.
typesIndex.ctaLine: Not sure which one is yours?

typeDetail.analysisTitle: What we've got on file for this absurd type

resultPage.actionsKicker: TAKE THIS FRONT PAGE WITH YOU

privacy.metaDescription: SBTI privacy policy. Learn how we handle your data — spoiler: we barely collect any.
privacy.introText: SBTI is a static website that runs entirely in your browser. We believe in minimal data collection — we only know what we absolutely need to keep the lights on.
privacy.section1Text: We use Google Analytics (ID: G-0182BSNWVS) to understand how people use SBTI. This includes: page views, approximate geographic location, device type, and browser information. We do NOT collect your quiz answers, personality results, or any personally identifiable information.
privacy.section2Text: All quiz logic runs locally in your browser using JavaScript. Your answers and results are never sent to any server. When you close or refresh the page, your quiz data is gone.
privacy.section3Text: Google Analytics may set cookies to distinguish users and track sessions. SBTI itself does not set any cookies. You can opt out of Google Analytics using browser extensions or privacy settings.
privacy.section4Text: We use the following third-party services: Google Analytics for usage statistics, Google Fonts for typography, and Cloudflare Pages for hosting.
privacy.section5Text: If you have questions about this privacy policy, you can reach us through our GitHub repository.

terms.metaDescription: SBTI terms of service. It's a fun personality test, not medical advice.
terms.section1Text: SBTI is a satirical personality test created for entertainment purposes only. It is NOT a psychological assessment, medical diagnosis, or professional personality evaluation. Do not use SBTI results to make important life decisions. Seriously.
terms.section2Text: The personality types, descriptions, and results provided by SBTI are humorous and satirical in nature. They should not be interpreted as psychological advice, counseling, or any form of professional guidance.
terms.section3Text: All content on SBTI, including personality type names, descriptions, images, and the quiz algorithm, is protected by copyright. You may share your results and link to SBTI pages, but you may not reproduce the quiz or content for commercial purposes without permission.
terms.section4Text: While SBTI's 15-dimension scoring system is mathematically sound, the personality types are designed to be humorous rather than scientifically validated. Any resemblance to your actual personality is purely coincidental (and hilarious).
terms.section5Text: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of SBTI after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

homepage.whatIsSbtiP1: SBTI (Satirical Behavior Type Indicator) is a humorous personality test that lovingly parodies MBTI. Instead of 16 generic types, we offer 27 absurdly specific personality archetypes — from CTRL (The Puppeteer) to DEAD (The Detached).
homepage.whatIsSbtiP2: Answer 30 fun questions, get matched to your personality type across 15 behavioral dimensions, and discover which hilarious archetype describes you best.
homepage.step1Desc: Quick questions about your daily habits and preferences. 3 options each, no overthinking required.
homepage.step2Desc: Our algorithm scores you across 5 models with 3 dimensions each, then finds your closest match.
homepage.step3Desc: Get your primary type, match percentage, dimension breakdown, and top 5 closest types.
homepage.faq1Q: Is SBTI a real personality test?
homepage.faq1A: It's a satirical test that uses real math. The 15-dimension scoring is legit — the personality descriptions are just hilariously honest.
homepage.faq2A: 3-5 minutes. 30 questions, 3 options each. Fast, fun, no sign-up.
homepage.faq3A: 100% free. No premium results, no email gates, no paywalls.
homepage.faq4A: Every type has a shareable page. Screenshot it, link it, or share directly to social media.
homepage.faq5A: Nope. Everything runs in your browser. We don't store your answers or results.

blog.inlineCta: Curious what you'd get? 31 questions, no sign-up.
blog.bottomCtaDesc: 31 questions, 1-3 minutes, find your SBTI personality type.

sbti-vs-mbti.title: SBTI vs MBTI: What's Actually Different?
sbti-vs-mbti.excerpt: One has 4 dimensions and 16 types. The other has 15 dimensions and 27 types. One makes you answer 93 questions. The other needs 31. The differences go deeper than question count.

27-personality-types.title: All 27 SBTI Personality Types: The Complete Guide
27-personality-types.excerpt: From CTRL the Controller to DEAD the Deadpan — every SBTI type with its code, name, and core traits in one place.

five-dimensions.title: The 5 Dimensions Behind SBTI: How the Test Works
five-dimensions.excerpt: Self, Emotion, Attitude, Action, Social — five facets, fifteen dimensions. Here's how SBTI uses 31 questions to build your personality profile.

sbti-vs-mbti.p1: SBTI and MBTI get compared constantly, but they're not even the same species. One wears a lab coat and tries to profile your psyche. The other wears a Hawaiian shirt and waits for you to screenshot the result.
sbti-vs-mbti.p2: That said, even though SBTI has been upfront about being a joke since day one, its design is more thoughtful than most "serious" viral quizzes. Let's break it down.
sbti-vs-mbti.origin_p1: MBTI stands for Myers-Briggs Type Indicator. It was developed in the 1940s by Katharine Briggs and her daughter Isabel Myers, based on Carl Jung's theory of psychological types. The psychology community is split on its validity, but that hasn't stopped it from showing up in every corporate training and social media bio.
sbti-vs-mbti.origin_p2: SBTI stands for Silly Behavioral Type Indicator. It was created by a Bilibili creator called 蛆肉儿串儿. Launched on April 9, 2026, it hit trending on every major Chinese platform within two days. It's not trying to replace MBTI or be a psychology tool. It's the thing you take so you can post the result.
sbti-vs-mbti.dim_p1: MBTI uses 4 binary dimensions (Extraversion/Introversion, Sensing/Intuition, Thinking/Feeling, Judging/Perceiving) to sort people into 16 types. SBTI uses 5 facets with 3 sub-dimensions each, so 15 dimensions in total, each scored on three levels (Low/Mid/High) and mapped to 27 types. Here's how they line up:
sbti-vs-mbti.types_p1: MBTI gives you a four-letter code like INFP or ESTJ with semi-formal labels like "Mediator" or "Executive." You could put it on a resume.
sbti-vs-mbti.types_p2: SBTI gives you things like DEAD (The Deadpan), MALO (The Monkey), FUCK (The Swearer), ATM-er (The Walking ATM). You probably wouldn't put these on a resume, but you'd absolutely post them on social media. That's the whole point: the result itself is the punchline.
sbti-vs-mbti.viral_p1: 31 questions, no registration, open and play, result in 1 to 3 minutes. The barrier is basically zero. MBTI asks 93 questions, and a lot of people close the tab halfway through.
sbti-vs-mbti.viral_p2: After you finish, you don't get a personality description that takes three paragraphs to parse. You get a label like "Monkey" or "Deadpan" with a meme-style illustration. Screenshot, share, and your friends want to take it too. Once that loop starts, it's hard to stop.
sbti-vs-mbti.viral_p3: The key ingredient: SBTI's self-deprecating humor hits exactly where young people are right now. In a world where everyone talks about burnout and "lying flat," a test that tells you "you're a monkey" actually makes people feel seen. You laugh, and then there's a split second of "damn, that's kind of true."
sbti-vs-mbti.which_p1: This question is a bit odd because the two tests are playing completely different games. You wouldn't ask "should I eat a meal or a snack" — they serve different needs.
sbti-vs-mbti.which_p2: Want to seriously explore your personality tendencies or plan your career? Take MBTI (or better yet, the Big Five). Want to drop something funny in the group chat? Take SBTI. They don't conflict.

27-personality-types.p1: SBTI has 25 standard personality types and 2 hidden ones. Each type has an English code, a Chinese nickname, and a set of personality descriptions that are sarcastic but weirdly accurate.
27-personality-types.p2: Here's a quick look at all 25 standard types. Click any one to jump to its full personality breakdown.
27-personality-types.p3: Beyond the 25 standard types, SBTI has 2 hidden types: HHHH and DRUNK. How to trigger them? You'll have to figure that out yourself.

five-dimensions.p1: Even though SBTI is a comedy test through and through, its dimension model is surprisingly well-structured. 5 facets, 15 sub-dimensions, each scored on three levels. Here's what each dimension actually measures.
five-dimensions.p2: One thing first: all the dimension names and descriptions carry SBTI's signature sarcasm. Don't read it like a psychology textbook — but you might find some descriptions hit closer to home than you'd expect.
five-dimensions.h2_overview: The five facets at a glance
five-dimensions.overview_p1: SBTI splits human behavior into five facets: Self Model (S), Emotion Model (E), Attitude Model (A), Action Drive Model (Ac), and Social Model (So). Each facet has 3 sub-dimensions, making 15 total. Every dimension is scored as Low (L), Mid (M), or High (H).
five-dimensions.dim_S_intro: How do you see yourself? How confident are you? Do you know what you want? These three sub-dimensions are all about your relationship with yourself.
five-dimensions.sub_S1_desc: Low scorers are harder on themselves than anyone else. Compliment them and they'll want to verify it first. High scorers have a solid read on who they are and don't get shaken by a stranger's remark.
five-dimensions.sub_S2_desc: Low means you're often buffering on the question "who am I." High means you're pretty clear on your temper, desires, and boundaries.
five-dimensions.sub_S3_desc: Low scorers prioritize comfort and safety. No need to run life on sprint mode. High scorers are easily propelled by goals and convictions.
five-dimensions.dim_E_intro: What's your state in relationships? Enough security? How much do you invest? Where are your boundaries?
five-dimensions.sub_E1_desc: Low scorers have sensitive alarms. A read receipt without a reply can trigger an entire worst-case screenplay. High scorers trust the relationship itself and don't panic at small disturbances.
five-dimensions.sub_E2_desc: Low scorers keep their heart on strict entry. Investment stays measured. High scorers go all in once they commit, pouring in both energy and emotion.
five-dimensions.sub_E3_desc: Low scorers cling easily and get clung to easily. High scorers need their space; even in close relationships, they keep a corner for themselves.
five-dimensions.dim_A_intro: How do you see the world? Suspicious first, or default trusting? Are rules guardrails or obstacles?
five-dimensions.sub_A1_desc: Low scorers see the world through a defensive filter: suspect first, approach later. High scorers default to believing in good faith and don't rush to condemn.
five-dimensions.sub_A2_desc: Low scorers bend rules when they can. Comfort and freedom come first. High scorers have a strong sense of order and prefer following process over improvising.
five-dimensions.sub_A3_desc: Low scorers tend to feel like a lot of things are just going through the motions. High scorers have more direction and roughly know where they're headed.
five-dimensions.dim_Ac_intro: What fuels your actions? Wanting to win, or wanting to not crash? Quick decision-maker? Strong executor?
five-dimensions.sub_Ac1_desc: Low scorers think about not crashing before anything else; their risk-avoidance system boots up faster than their ambition. High scorers light up at the prospect of progress and growth.
five-dimensions.sub_Ac2_desc: Low scorers spin around a few times before deciding. Internal meetings frequently run over. High scorers make the call fast and don't like looking back.
five-dimensions.sub_Ac3_desc: Low scorers have a deep bond with deadlines. The later it gets, the closer they are to awakening. High scorers have strong drive to push things forward; unfinished work feels like a splinter.
five-dimensions.dim_So_intro: What role do you play in groups? Do you reach out first or wait? Clear boundaries? Straight talker?
five-dimensions.sub_So1_desc: Low scorers are slow starters socially. Initiating takes half a day of building up courage. High scorers are more willing to open the floor and don't mind being noticed.
five-dimensions.sub_So2_desc: Low scorers want closeness and merging; once familiar, people get pulled into the inner circle fast. High scorers have strong boundaries and instinctively step back if someone gets too close.
five-dimensions.sub_So3_desc: Low scorers are more direct. What's inside basically comes out. High scorers are skilled at switching modes for different contexts, distributing authenticity in layers.
five-dimensions.calc_p1: Each of the 31 questions maps to one or more dimensions. Each answer option gives a score of 1 to 3 (corresponding to L/M/H). After you finish, the system aggregates your scores across all 15 dimensions into a pattern string, then matches it against whichever of the 27 types is closest.
five-dimensions.calc_p2: The matching algorithm doesn't require an exact match. It finds the type with the shortest distance to your pattern, so people with the same type can still have different trait profiles. The result page shows your match percentage with that type.
five-dimensions.disclaimer_p1: SBTI has zero scientific validity. The same person could get a different result three times in one day. That's not a bug — it's a feature. This is a thing you take so you can post the result, not a career planning tool. If it's fun, it's working.

compat.title: SBTI Compatibility Check
compat.description: Two SBTI types walk into a bar... how does it end?

fated.label: A Match Made in Heaven
fated.desc: The universe clearly has a shipping agenda here. These two are so compatible it's almost suspicious — like fate swiped right before they even met.

sync.label: In Perfect Sync
sync.desc: Finishing each other's sentences, sharing the same chaotic energy. Not soulmates, but dangerously close — one group chat away from a cult.

spicy.label: Love-Hate Dynamic
spicy.desc: Explosively good and explosively bad, sometimes in the same afternoon. Like chilli chocolate — you can't stop, but you're not sure you should start.

plastic.label: Plastic Friendship
plastic.desc: The kind of friends who smile warmly and immediately forget each other's birthdays. Civility is their love language. It works, mostly.

awkward.label: Awkward Energy
awkward.desc: Every conversation has at least two silences that last three years. Not enemies — just two humans who accidentally ended up in the same timeline.

disaster.label: Mutual Destruction
disaster.desc: Together, these two could not plan a picnic without starting a geopolitical incident. Individually fine. Together: a controlled demolition that forgot the 'controlled' part.
